 {"id":"bd-14la","title":"Allocation Budget: Sequential Leak Detection (e-process + CUSUM)","description":"## Goal
Detect allocation regressions/leaks in reflow with formal, anytime-valid guarantees and actionable diagnostics.

## Core Idea (Alien Artifact)
Track allocation counts/bytes per frame as a time series. Use:
- CUSUM for quick drift detection (mean shift).
- An e-process for anytime-valid testing to avoid false positives under optional stopping.

Example e-process for residuals r_t (mean 0 under null):
E_t = product exp(lambda * r_t - (lambda^2 * sigma^2)/2). Trigger if E_t > 1/alpha.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Detect sustained allocation drift within <= N frames for target deltas.
- False positive rate <= alpha on stable runs.
- Debug output includes evidence ledger: r_t, E_t, thresholds, and suspected callsites.

## Tests (REQUIRED)
- unit_eprocess_threshold: triggers at E_t > 1/alpha.
- unit_cusum_detects_shift: mean shift detected under drift.\n- property_fpr_control: empirical FPR <= alpha + tolerance.

## E2E (REQUIRED)
- Synthetic leak injection: verify detection and logs.
- Stable run: no alerts, E_t stays bounded.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-02T23:14:05.146984605Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-03T02:54:32.204779375Z","closed_at":"2026-02-03T02:54:32.204757774Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-14s3","title":"Implement VirtualizedList widget with overscan","description":"# Implement VirtualizedList widget with overscan

## Background
Part of Progressive Rendering (bd-3t87). Widget that renders only visible items from a virtualized container.

## Technical Design

```rust
/// Virtualized list widget
pub struct VirtualizedList<T: RenderItem> {
    /// The virtualized data
    data: Virtualized<T>,
    
    /// Item renderer
    render_item: Box<dyn Fn(&T, Rect, &mut Buffer, &RenderBudget)>,
    
    /// Selection state
    selected: Option<usize>,
    
    /// Scrollbar style
    scrollbar_style: ScrollbarStyle,
    
    /// Whether to show scrollbar
    show_scrollbar: bool,
}

/// Trait for items that can render themselves
pub trait RenderItem {
    fn render(&self, area: Rect, buf: &mut Buffer, budget: &RenderBudget);
    fn height(&self) -> u16 { 1 }
}

impl<T: RenderItem> Widget for VirtualizedList<T> {
    fn render(&self, area: Rect, buf: &mut Buffer, budget: &RenderBudget) {
        // Reserve space for scrollbar if needed
        let content_width = if self.show_scrollbar && self.data.len() > self.data.visible_count {
            area.width.saturating_sub(1)
        } else {
            area.width
        };
        
        let content_area = Rect::new(area.x, area.y, content_width, area.height);
        
        // Get render range with overscan
        let range = self.data.render_range(area.height);
        
        // Calculate starting Y position
        let first_visible = self.data.scroll_offset;
        let offset_in_range = first_visible.saturating_sub(range.start);
        let mut y = area.y.saturating_sub((offset_in_range as u16) * self.item_height());
        
        // Render each item in range
        for idx in range.clone() {
            if y >= area.bottom() {
                break;
            }
            
            let item_height = match &self.data.item_height {
                ItemHeight::Fixed(h) => *h,
                ItemHeight::Variable(cache) => cache.get(idx),
            };
            
            // Skip if completely above viewport
            if y + item_height <= area.y {
                y += item_height;
                continue;
            }
            
            // Calculate visible portion
            let visible_top = y.max(area.y);
            let visible_bottom = (y + item_height).min(area.bottom());
            let visible_height = visible_bottom.saturating_sub(visible_top);
            
            if visible_height > 0 {
                let item_area = Rect::new(
                    area.x,
                    visible_top,
                    content_width,
                    visible_height,
                );
                
                // Highlight selected
                let is_selected = self.selected == Some(idx);
                if is_selected {
                    buf.fill_rect(item_area, Cell::new(' ').bg(Color::DarkGray));
                }
                
                // Render item
                if let Some(item) = self.data.get(idx) {
                    item.render(item_area, buf, budget);
                }
            }
            
            y += item_height;
        }
        
        // Render scrollbar
        if self.show_scrollbar && self.data.len() > self.data.visible_count {
            self.render_scrollbar(area, buf);
        }
    }
}

impl<T: RenderItem> VirtualizedList<T> {
    fn render_scrollbar(&self, area: Rect, buf: &mut Buffer) {
        let scrollbar_x = area.right() - 1;
        
        // Calculate thumb position and size
        let total = self.data.len() as f32;
        let visible = self.data.visible_count as f32;
        let offset = self.data.scroll_offset as f32;
        
        let thumb_size = ((visible / total) * area.height as f32).max(1.0) as u16;
        let thumb_pos = ((offset / total) * area.height as f32) as u16;
        
        // Draw track
        for y in area.y..area.bottom() {
            buf.set_cell(scrollbar_x, y, Cell::new('│').fg(Color::DarkGray));
        }
        
        // Draw thumb
        for y in area.y + thumb_pos..area.y + thumb_pos + thumb_size {
            if y < area.bottom() {
                buf.set_cell(scrollbar_x, y, Cell::new('█').fg(Color::Gray));
            }
        }
    }
    
    fn item_height(&self) -> u16 {
        match &self.data.item_height {
            ItemHeight::Fixed(h) => *h,
            ItemHeight::Variable(_) => 1,  // Default for calculation
        }
    }
}
```

- [ ] Scrollbar shows accurate position
- [ ] Selection highlighting works

 {"id":"bd-16k","title":"Unicode width corpus testing with WTF-8","description":"TESTING - UNICODE WIDTH CORPUS

## Background
Unicode width calculation is notoriously complex with edge cases around emoji, CJK, combining characters, and ZWJ sequences. A comprehensive test corpus is essential for correctness.

## Technical Requirements
- Test corpus categories:
  - Basic ASCII (width 1)
  - CJK Unified Ideographs (width 2)
  - Fullwidth ASCII variants (width 2)
  - Halfwidth Katakana (width 1)
  - Emoji with modifiers (width varies)
  - ZWJ sequences (flag emoji, family emoji)
  - Combining characters (width 0)
  - Control characters (width 0)
  - Ambiguous width characters (context-dependent)
- WTF-8 handling for unpaired surrogates
- Terminal-specific width (vs Unicode Standard)
- Test against unicode-width crate results

## Known Edge Cases
- 👨‍👩‍👧‍👦 (family emoji ZWJ) - varies by terminal
- 🏳️‍🌈 (flag ZWJ) - often renders as 2 cells
- Regional indicators (flag pairs)
- Variation selectors (VS15/VS16)

## Design Rationale
Correctness in width calculation directly impacts rendering quality. The corpus captures real-world edge cases that simple tests miss.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Corpus with 1000+ test cases
- [ ] Categorized by width type
- [ ] Known terminal-specific behaviors documented
- [ ] Property tests for grapheme iteration
- [ ] Fuzz testing for malformed input
- [ ] Benchmark for width calculation performance

 {"id":"bd-1qpv","title":"Implement ErrorBoundary<W> wrapper widget","description":"# Implement ErrorBoundary<W> wrapper widget

## Background
Part of Widget Error Boundaries (bd-1euu). The core wrapper that catches panics and renders fallback.

## Technical Design

```rust
use std::panic::{catch_unwind, AssertUnwindSafe};

/// Widget that catches panics from inner widget
pub struct ErrorBoundary<W: Widget> {
    /// The wrapped widget
    inner: W,
    
    /// Widget name for error reporting
    name: &'static str,
    
    /// Current state
    state: ErrorBoundaryState,
    
    /// Configuration
    config: ErrorBoundaryConfig,
}

/// Error boundary state machine
enum ErrorBoundaryState {
    /// Widget is healthy
    Healthy,
    
    /// Widget has failed, showing fallback
    Failed(CapturedError),
    
    /// Attempting recovery
    Recovering {
        attempts: u32,
        last_error: CapturedError,
    },
}

impl<W: Widget> ErrorBoundary<W> {
    /// Wrap a widget in an error boundary
    pub fn new(inner: W, name: &'static str) -> Self {
        Self {
            inner,
            name,
            state: ErrorBoundaryState::Healthy,
            config: ErrorBoundaryConfig::default(),
        }
    }
    
    /// Configure max recovery attempts
    pub fn with_max_recovery(mut self, max: u32) -> Self {
        self.config.max_recovery_attempts = max;
        self
    }
    
    /// Configure custom fallback factory
    pub fn with_fallback<F>(mut self, factory: F) -> Self
    where
        F: Fn(&CapturedError) -> Box<dyn Widget> + 'static,
    {
        self.config.fallback_factory = Some(Box::new(factory));
        self
    }
    
    /// Check if in error state
    pub fn is_failed(&self) -> bool {
        matches\!(self.state, ErrorBoundaryState::Failed(_))
    }
    
    /// Get current error if any
    pub fn error(&self) -> Option<&CapturedError> {
        match &self.state {
            ErrorBoundaryState::Failed(e) => Some(e),
            ErrorBoundaryState::Recovering { last_error, .. } => Some(last_error),
            ErrorBoundaryState::Healthy => None,
        }
    }
}

impl<W: Widget> Widget for ErrorBoundary<W> {
    fn render(&mut self, area: Rect, buf: &mut Buffer, budget: &RenderBudget) {
        match &self.state {
            ErrorBoundaryState::Healthy => {
                // Try to render inner widget
                let result = catch_unwind(AssertUnwindSafe(|| {
                    self.inner.render(area, buf, budget)
                }));
                
                if let Err(panic) = result {
                    let error = CapturedError::from_panic(
                        panic,
                        self.name,
                        area,
                        RenderPhase::Render,
                    );
                    self.handle_error(error, area, buf);
                }
            }
            ErrorBoundaryState::Failed(error) => {
                self.render_fallback(error, area, buf);
            }
            ErrorBoundaryState::Recovering { last_error, .. } => {
                // Try render, but have fallback ready
                let result = catch_unwind(AssertUnwindSafe(|| {
                    self.inner.render(area, buf, budget)
                }));
                
                match result {
                    Ok(()) => {
                        // Recovery successful\!
                        self.state = ErrorBoundaryState::Healthy;
                        tracing::info\!(widget = self.name, "Widget recovered");
                    }
                    Err(panic) => {
                        let error = CapturedError::from_panic(
                            panic,
                            self.name,
                            area,
                            RenderPhase::Render,
                        );
                        self.handle_recovery_failure(error, area, buf);
                    }
                }
            }
        }
    }
    
    fn is_essential(&self) -> bool {
        self.inner.is_essential()
    }
}

impl<W: Widget> ErrorBoundary<W> {
    fn handle_error(&mut self, error: CapturedError, area: Rect, buf: &mut Buffer) {
        if self.config.log_errors {
            tracing::error\!(
                widget = error.widget_name,
                message = %error.message,
                area = ?error.area,
                "Widget panicked"
            );
        }
        
        self.state = ErrorBoundaryState::Failed(error.clone());
        self.render_fallback(&error, area, buf);
    }
    
    fn handle_recovery_failure(&mut self, error: CapturedError, area: Rect, buf: &mut Buffer) {
        if let ErrorBoundaryState::Recovering { attempts, .. } = &mut self.state {
            *attempts += 1;
            if *attempts >= self.config.max_recovery_attempts {
                // Give up, stay in failed state
                self.state = ErrorBoundaryState::Failed(error.clone());
                tracing::warn\!(
                    widget = self.name,
                    attempts = self.config.max_recovery_attempts,
                    "Widget recovery failed, giving up"
                );
            }
        }
        self.render_fallback(&error, area, buf);
    }
}
```

 {"id":"bd-1rqf","title":"Migrate interactive widgets with hit testing (Category B)","description":"## Scope

Migrate Category B widgets - those that need mouse interaction via hit testing.

## Frame Features Used

- `frame.hit_grid` - Register clickable regions for mouse events

## Migration Pattern

1. Change signature to use Frame
2. For clickable regions, add hit grid registration:
   ```rust
   fn render(&self, area: Rect, frame: &mut Frame) {
       let buf = &mut frame.buffer;
       
       // ... draw the widget ...
       
       // Register clickable region
       if let Some(hit_grid) = &mut frame.hit_grid {
           hit_grid.register(area, HitId::Widget(self.id));
       }
   }
   ```

## Widgets Expected in This Category

Based on functionality (to be confirmed by audit):
- List (selectable items)
- Table (clickable rows/cells)
- Buttons (if any)
- Links (if any)

## Design Consideration

Some widgets may have multiple clickable regions (e.g., table cells).
These need careful hit ID assignment for disambiguation.

## Important

- Preserve existing behavior for non-mouse interaction
- Hit testing is ADDITIVE - widgets must still work without hit_grid
- Check `if let Some(hit_grid)` - don't assume it's always present

## Verification

- `cargo check -p ftui-widgets` must pass
- All existing tests must pass
- Manual verification of mouse interaction (if test harness supports it)","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-01T23:22:12.321561652Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-01T23:31:26.711750892Z","closed_at":"2026-02-01T23:28:51.718180201Z","close_reason":"Added hit testing to List and Table widgets. Make resize/reflow first-class: zero-flicker, atomic rendering, and deterministic behavior under resize storms.

## Scope
- Core reflow engine with atomic present + shrink cleanup.
- Resize stream coalescing + scheduler (adaptive + anytime-valid).
- Responsive layout primitives (breakpoints + auto UI height).
- Instrumentation and performance budgets.

## Deliverables
- Reflow pipeline changes in core/render/runtime.
- Formal scheduler model + decision rules.
- Test harnesses (unit, property, snapshot, E2E PTY).

## Tests / Validation
- Unit/property tests for buffer invariants and scheduler correctness.
- Snapshot tests for reflow stability across sizes.
- E2E PTY resize-storm scripts with verbose JSONL logging (size, timing, seed, checksums).
- Performance baselines (p50/p95/p99) with budgets and regression gates.

## Acceptance Criteria
- No flicker/ghosting on resize.
- Deterministic output under fixed inputs.
- Documented budgets and failure modes.

## Success Criteria
- [ ] The resize scheduler has an explicit formal model (loss matrix + decision rule) and is implemented without hidden heuristics.
- [ ] Resize-storm PTY E2E tests produce high-signal JSONL logs and deterministic replays (seeded).
- [ ] Golden checksums + snapshots verify behavior across a size matrix (80x24, 120x40, and at least one tiny terminal case).

- PTY E2E tests with resize storms and verbose logs.

 {"id":"bd-2ss","title":"Document Windows v1 Limitations and Compatibility","description":"# Document Windows v1 Limitations and Compatibility

## Background
Plan Section ADR-004 and Section 0.6.6 require a "Windows limitations" section that is honest about what works and what doesn't.

## Document Location
`docs/WINDOWS.md` or `docs/compatibility/windows.md`

## Required Content

### 1. Supported Features (v1)
Document what IS expected to work on Windows:
- [ ] Raw mode enter/exit and cleanup on panic
- [ ] Key input handling (basic keys, modifiers)
- [ ] Resize events
- [ ] Basic mouse support (where Crossterm supports it)
- [ ] Color output:
  - 16 colors (always)
  - 256 colors (Windows Terminal, modern ConHost)
  - TrueColor (Windows Terminal)

### 2. Known Limitations (v1)
Be honest about what MAY NOT work or is best-effort:
- [ ] DEC synchronized output (mode 2026): Not widely supported
- [ ] OSC 8 hyperlinks: Windows Terminal only (cmd.exe/ConHost: no)
- [ ] Bracketed paste: Varies by terminal
- [ ] Focus events: May not work in all environments
- [ ] Kitty keyboard protocol: Limited support
- [ ] Scroll-region optimization: Behavior varies
- [ ] Mouse SGR mode: May fall back to legacy encoding

### 3. Terminal Compatibility Matrix (Windows)
| Feature | Windows Terminal | cmd.exe | ConHost | PowerShell |
|---------|------------------|---------|---------|------------|
| TrueColor | ✓ | ✗ | ~ | depends |
| OSC 8 Links | ✓ | ✗ | ✗ | ~ |
| Mouse | ✓ | ✗ | ~ | ~ |
| Sync Output | ✗ | ✗ | ✗ | ✗ |

### 4. Configuration Recommendations
- Recommend Windows Terminal for best experience
- Document any required ConPTY settings
- Explain TERM/COLORTERM environment handling on Windows

### 5. Troubleshooting Section
Common Windows issues:
- "Colors don't show": Check terminal emulator
- "Mouse not working": Enable in terminal settings
- "Cleanup not working": Known ConHost limitation
- "Unicode display broken": Font/codepage issues

### 6. Future Improvements
List what may improve in future versions:
- Better ConHost support
- WSL integration notes
- Specific terminal emulator enhancements

## Cross-References
- Link to ADR-004 for decision context
- Link to terminal compatibility matrix (bd-1un)
- Link to capability detection implementation

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] WINDOWS.md exists with all sections
- [ ] Honest about limitations (not marketing fluff)
- [ ] Tested on actual Windows systems
- [ ] Includes actionable troubleshooting
- [ ] Linked from main README.md

 {"id":"bd-2tyd","title":"Implement CachedWidget<W> wrapper","description":"# Implement CachedWidget<W> wrapper

## Background
Part of Frame Caching (bd-6qxh). Cache rendered widget output to avoid re-rendering static content.

## Technical Design

```rust
/// Widget wrapper that caches rendered output
pub struct CachedWidget<W: Widget> {
    /// Inner widget
    inner: W,
    /// Cached buffer
    cache: Option<CachedBuffer>,
    /// Last rendered area
    last_area: Option<Rect>,
    /// Dirty flag (needs re-render)
    dirty: bool,
    /// Cache key (for invalidation)
    cache_key: u64,
}

/// Cached buffer with metadata
struct CachedBuffer {
    buffer: Buffer,
    hash: u64,
    rendered_at: Instant,
}

impl<W: Widget> CachedWidget<W> {
    pub fn new(inner: W) -> Self {
        Self {
            inner,
            cache: None,
            last_area: None,
            dirty: true,
            cache_key: 0,
        }
    }
    
    /// Mark cache as dirty (needs re-render)
    pub fn mark_dirty(&mut self) {
        self.dirty = true;
    }
    
    /// Invalidate if model hash changed
    pub fn invalidate_if_changed(&mut self, new_hash: u64) {
        if self.cache_key \!= new_hash {
            self.dirty = true;
            self.cache_key = new_hash;
        }
    }
    
    /// Get inner widget
    pub fn inner(&self) -> &W {
        &self.inner
    }
    
    /// Get mutable inner widget (marks dirty)
    pub fn inner_mut(&mut self) -> &mut W {
        self.dirty = true;
        &mut self.inner
    }
}

impl<W: Widget> Widget for CachedWidget<W> {
    fn render(&mut self, area: Rect, buf: &mut Buffer, budget: &RenderBudget) {
        // Check if we need to re-render
        let needs_render = self.dirty
            || self.last_area \!= Some(area)
            || self.cache.is_none();
        
        if needs_render {
            // Render to cache buffer
            let mut cache_buf = Buffer::empty(area);
            self.inner.render(area, &mut cache_buf, budget);
            
            self.cache = Some(CachedBuffer {
                buffer: cache_buf,
                hash: self.cache_key,
                rendered_at: Instant::now(),
            });
            self.last_area = Some(area);
            self.dirty = false;
            
            tracing::debug\!(
                widget = std::any::type_name::<W>(),
                "Cache miss - re-rendered"
            );
        } else {
            tracing::trace\!(
                widget = std::any::type_name::<W>(),
                "Cache hit"
            );
        }
        
        // Composite cache to output
        if let Some(cache) = &self.cache {
            buf.merge(&cache.buffer);
        }
    }
    
    fn is_essential(&self) -> bool {
        self.inner.is_essential()
    }
}
```

## When to Use
- Static headers/footers
- Help text
- Fixed decorations
- Any widget that changes infrequently

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] CachedWidget wraps any Widget
- [ ] Cache used when not dirty
- [ ] Area change triggers re-render
- [ ] mark_dirty() works
- [ ] invalidate_if_changed() for model sync","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-01T03:24:11.920190156Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-01T21:10:11.632556566Z","closed_at":"2026-02-01T21:10:11.632536388Z","close_reason":"CachedWidget wrapper already implemented in crates/ftui-widgets/src/cached.rs with tests","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2tyd","depends_on_id":"bd-6qxh","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-03T14:32:17Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-2u4","title":"Implement snapshot/golden testing framework","description":"TESTING - SNAPSHOT TESTS

## Background
Snapshot testing captures expected Buffer output and compares against future runs. Essential for preventing visual regressions and validating rendering correctness.

## Technical Requirements
- Snapshot capture:
  - Buffer → text representation (with ANSI or stripped)
  - Buffer → binary serialization
  - Configurable normalization (strip cursor position, etc.)
- Snapshot storage:
  - tests/snapshots/ directory
  - Filename based on test name + variant
  - Human-readable format for review
- Comparison:
  - Exact match mode
  - Fuzzy match mode (ignore whitespace variations)
  - Diff output on failure showing changes
- Update workflow:
  - BLESS=1 env var to update snapshots
  - cargo insta integration optional

## Design Rationale
Visual output is hard to assert with unit tests. Snapshots capture 'known good' output and flag any changes for human review. Prevents subtle rendering regressions.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Snapshot capture API
- [ ] Text and binary formats
- [ ] Diff output on mismatch
- [ ] Update/bless workflow
- [ ] Integration with cargo test
- [ ] Document snapshot review process
- [ ] Sample snapshots for core widgets

## Integration
- Consider insta crate for snapshot management
- Support parallel test execution
- Snapshot files committed to git

 {"id":"bd-2ua8","title":"Migrate ErrorBoundary widget (error_boundary.rs)","description":"## Scope

Migrate the ErrorBoundary widget in crates/ftui-widgets/src/error_boundary.rs.

## Special Considerations

ErrorBoundary wraps other widgets and handles render panics gracefully.
It has multiple implementations:

1. **ErrorBoundary<W>** - Generic wrapper for any widget
2. **FallbackWidget** - Widget shown when wrapped widget panics
3. **CustomErrorBoundary<W>** - ErrorBoundary with custom fallback

## Error Handling with Frame

When the wrapped widget panics during render, ErrorBoundary needs to:
1. Catch the panic
2. Show fallback content
3. NOT corrupt Frame state

Consideration: If wrapped widget partially modifies frame.hit_grid or
frame.cursor_position before panicking, should we roll back?

## Migration Approach

```rust
impl<W: Widget> StatefulWidget for ErrorBoundary<W> {
    fn render(&self, area: Rect, frame: &mut Frame, state: &mut ErrorBoundaryState) {
        // Save Frame state for potential rollback
        let saved_cursor = frame.cursor_position;
        let saved_cursor_vis = frame.cursor_visible;
        
        // Try to render wrapped widget
        let result = std::panic::catch_unwind(AssertUnwindSafe(|| {
            self.inner.render(area, frame);
        }));
        
        match result {
            Ok(()) => { /* success */ }
            Err(panic) => {
                // Restore Frame state
                frame.cursor_position = saved_cursor;
                frame.cursor_visible = saved_cursor_vis;
                // Note: hit_grid cleanup is complex, may need to just skip
                
                // Render fallback
                self.fallback.render(area, frame);
                state.has_error = true;
            }
        }
    }
}
```

## Important

- Error boundary must be robust - it's the last line of defense
- Consider what Frame state needs rollback vs what can be left
- Test panic handling thoroughly

## Verification

- Panic in wrapped widget correctly shows fallback
- Frame state not corrupted after panic
